This kernel appears to be a synthetic/contrived microbenchmark rather than something from a known codebase or tutorial. The specific characteristics — the magic constants (17, 4095, 257, 8 iterations, 1.1f multiplier), the bitwise AND masking for pseudo-random access patterns within a 4096-element window, and the simple multiply-accumulate loop — look like a hand-crafted test kernel designed to stress particular aspects of memory access patterns or arithmetic throughput. It doesn't match any well-known benchmark suite (e.g., Rodinia, Parboil, SHOC) or standard CUDA tutorial code that I'm aware of.
